description: Controller can operate as a PCIe Root Complex to enumerate and manage downstream endpoints. Driver programs DEVSTAT.PCIESSMODE to RC, configures BARs/capabilities, and initiates link training. Enumeration and remote config windows are used after link-up for device discovery and setup.
dependencies: Module power/clock enabled; DEVSTAT.PCIESSMODE=RC; SerDes configured and locked; CMD_STATUS.LTSSM_EN

description: Controller supports single-function PCIe Endpoint operation. Driver sets EP mode, programs BAR apertures and capabilities, and handles inbound requests via inbound translation regions. MSI or legacy interrupts signal events to the host.
dependencies: Module power/clock; DEVSTAT.PCIESSMODE=EP; BARs and IB translation configured

description: Supports PCIe Gen1 and Gen2 data rates per lane. Driver configures SerDes PLL and Port Logic Gen2 parameters then trains the link at the negotiated speed. Proper clocking and equalization ensure stable operation across rates.
dependencies: Stable reference clock; SerDes PLL lock; PL_GEN2 configured

feature_name: x1/x2 link width with dynamic width conversion
description: Operates with one or two lanes and can downtrain dynamically if fewer lanes are available. Negotiation during link training selects the active width. No software splitting of x2 into two independent x1 links is supported.
dependencies: SerDes lanes wired; proper pinmux; link training enabled

description: Hardware supports automatic lane reversal to accommodate board routing swaps. This feature engages during link initialization without explicit software control. Driver simply enables link training and validates negotiated state.
dependencies: SerDes lanes connected; LTSSM enabled

description: Receiver polarity inversion can be handled to tolerate differential pair flips. Configuration or automatic handling ensures correct data reception. Driver verifies link quality and adjusts only if required.
dependencies: SerDes configured; link training/diagnostics available

feature_name: Single Virtual Channel and Traffic Class
description: Implements a single VC and single TC simplifying QoS management. Driver does not configure multiple virtual channels and should align traffic expectations accordingly. Credit management is automatic.
dependencies: Hardware fixed capability; standard PCIe config space

description: Hardware limits outbound payload to 128 bytes, inbound to 256 bytes, and remote read request size to 256 bytes. Driver sets MPS/MRRS in Device Control accordingly and sizes DMA transactions to avoid segmentation. Exceeding limits reduces throughput due to extra TLPs.
dependencies: PCIe Capability Device Control; application limits documented

description: Supports PCIe AER with correctable/uncorrectable status and optional ECRC generation/check. Driver enables reporting/masking as required and may disable ECRC for peers lacking support. Error ISR collects logs and clears status.
dependencies: AER capability; PCIE_ACCR; PCIE_CERR/UNCERR and masks

description: Supports Active State Power Management states L0s and L1 to save power. Driver coordinates ASPM policy with link partners and platform latency constraints. L2 state is not supported and must not be requested.
dependencies: PCIe PM capability; PMCFG; platform policy

feature_name: Legacy INTx support (RC receive / EP generate)
description: Supports legacy INTx signaling: RC receives and services, EP can assert towards RC. Driver configures legacy interrupt enable/clear and routes events to the SoC interrupt controller.
dependencies: LEGACY_* IRQ regs; SoC interrupt routing; IRQ_EOI

description: Supports Message Signaled Interrupts for efficient event delivery. Endpoint driver programs MSI address/data and triggers writes; RC driver enables MSI reception and dispatches vectors. Prefer MSI over INTx for performance.
dependencies: MSI capability; MSI*_IRQ_ENABLE_SET/CLR; SoC IRQ routing

description: Provides PHY loopback for link validation and board bring-up. Driver enables loopback in RC mode, observes LTSSM/debug registers, and verifies error counters for integrity.
dependencies: Port Logic/PHY loopback controls; DEBUG0/1

description: Hardware translates SoC addresses to PCIe memory/config/I/O TLPs via outbound windows. Driver sizes windows with OB_SIZE and programs offsets/attributes, then enables OB_XLT for access to remote targets.
dependencies: OB_SIZE; OB_OFFSET_INDEX/HI; CMD_STATUS.OB_XLT_EN

description: Up to four inbound regions map PCIe addresses into SoC memory space. Driver programs BAR match, start, and offset registers and enables IB_XLT to accept inbound TLPs safely. Protects memory with precise aperture sizing.
dependencies: IB_BARn; IB_STARTn_LO/HI; IB_OFFSETn; CMD_STATUS.IB_XLT_EN

description: In RC mode, dedicated windows allow software to access downstream devices' config and I/O spaces. Driver selects bus/device/function in CFG_SETUP and performs 32-bit single accesses after link-up.
dependencies: CFG_SETUP; IOBASE; 32-bit non-burst access rules

description: Implements BAR configuration for EP and filtering for RC/EP transactions. Driver sets BAR sizes/types and uses filtering to block invalid accesses. DBI CS2 enables BAR mask programming when required.
dependencies: BAR registers; CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2; filtering controls

description: Supports completion timeout detection and selective filtering of posted/non-posted/completions. Driver configures timeouts and filters to harden against misbehaving peers and to aid recovery.
dependencies: Completion timeout controls; filtering registers

description: Byte swapping supported to match big-endian system memory layout. Driver programs ENDIAN register for inbound/outbound paths to ensure correct data interpretation. Validate with known patterns before enabling DMA.
dependencies: ENDIAN register; big-endian swap tables

description: Master transactions can carry privilege and priority tags. Driver sets MST_PRIV, PRIVID, and priority to enforce access control and QoS policies on the SoC interconnect.
dependencies: PRIORITY register (MST_PRIV, PRIVID, MST_PRIORITY)

description: Outgoing TLPs can be marked with Relaxed Ordering and No Snoop. Driver sets TLPCFG bits to tune performance where ordering and cache coherency allow. Use with caution across bridges and non-coherent endpoints.
dependencies: TLPCFG.RELAXED; TLPCFG.NO_SNOOP

feature_name: LTSSM control and link status debug
description: Software controls link training and monitors LTSSM state for bring-up and reliability. Driver asserts LTSSM_EN to train and polls DEBUG0 for state L0. Additional debug registers aid fault isolation.
dependencies: CMD_STATUS.LTSSM_EN; Port Logic DEBUG0/1

description: Enables issuing a downstream port reset from software for recovery. Driver deasserts FLUSH_N as required, sets RSTCMD.INIT_RST, and waits for link re-training before resuming traffic.
dependencies: RSTCMD.INIT_RST; FLUSH_N handling; LTSSM state

description: Provides status bits to indicate non-empty inbound or outbound buffers. Driver may poll for diagnostics and quiesce flows prior to resets or reconfiguration. Not typically required in steady-state operation.
dependencies: ACT_STATUS.IB_NOT_EMPTY/OB_NOT_EMPTY

feature_name: No integrated DMA engine (use SoC EDMA)
description: Controller lacks an internal DMA engine; data movement uses SoC DMA over VBUSM. Driver configures EDMA channels/PaRAM and aligns transactions with PCIe payload limits for efficiency.
dependencies: EDMA available on SoC; VBUSM master/slave ports

description: Inbound I/O TLPs are not accepted in either RC or EP mode. Software must avoid mapping I/O spaces for inbound access and use memory space instead. RC still issues outbound I/O if needed.
dependencies: Design limitation per spec; IO windows outbound only

feature_name: Single link; no switch or x2 split
description: Implements a single PCIe link and cannot act as a switch or split a x2 link into independent x1 links. System designs must attach a single device or a downstream switch externally if fan-out is required.
dependencies: Hardware topology constraint; SerDes lane bonding

description: Supports device power management states except D3 cold with auxiliary power. Driver coordinates PM messages and ensures proper context save/restore across L1 entries. Avoid reliance on Vaux retention.
dependencies: PM capability; PMCMD/PMCFG; platform power rails

description: Requires programming the SerDes PLL and verifying lock prior to training. On KeyStone I, chip-level SerDes registers need Kicker unlock before writes. Driver sequences enable, delay, and lock polling.
dependencies: PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL; PCIE_SERDES_STS.LOCK; Kicker unlock (KeyStone I)

description: Captures error sources with first error pointers and masks to control reporting. Driver services ISRs, reads AER logs, clears status, and can throttle traffic while recovering. Integrate with system health monitoring.
dependencies: AER regs; PCIE_CERR/UNCERR and MASK regs
